description_long: This is a large, roughly hewn stone block with a semi-cylindrical profile and a prominent carved groove pattern on its curved face. The stone appears to be made of coarse-grained granite or similar material, with a light gray coloration and visible mineral flecks. The curved side features two concentric, deeply incised arcs, suggesting it may have served as part of an architectural or infrastructural element, such as a curb or wheel stop. The flat sides are irregular and rough, with one side showing a distinct horizontal indentation, possibly from wear or intentional shaping. The surface is weathered, with minor chips and abrasions, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor conditions. Its substantial mass and robust construction make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments.
description: A large, semi-cylindrical stone block with carved grooves and a rough, weathered surface, likely used as an urban barrier or curb.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broad, curved face with two prominent carved arcs and a rough, granular texture.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the semi-circular profile and the depth of the carved grooves, with the stone's thickness visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the flat, irregularly shaped back with a horizontal indentation and rough, uncarved surface.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the semi-circular profile and carved grooves, emphasizing the stone's curvature and mass.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or aligned in a row as a barrier
attribute_pattern: Two deeply carved, concentric arcs on the curved face, with irregular granular texture elsewhere
attribute_weathering: Rough and abraded with minor chips, indicating moderate weathering and outdoor exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may be partially embedded in concrete or soil, often aligned with other stones
